executives.name: Benny McKee
executives.vitals: Dallas Baptist University (BS Biology); HACCP & Food Safety Certified.
executives.bio: With nearly three decades of executive leadership in the laboratory sector, Benny drives the operational efficiency and customer-centric culture at BioChroma. He ensures that laboratory throughput never compromises the personalized service required by high-growth brands.

executives.name: Dr. Cheri Turman-Ballard
executives.vitals: PhD Biomedical Sciences (UT MD Anderson); MS/BS Chemistry (SFA).
executives.bio: A distinguished pharmaceutical scientist with over 25 years of cross-functional experience. Dr. Ballard provides the scientific stewardship for BioChroma, overseeing complex method development and ensuring all analytical pathways meet the highest peer-reviewed standards.

executives.name: Amber Howeth
executives.bio: Amber brings over 20 years of expertise in financial management for the life sciences sector. She oversees fiscal precision, contract management, and sustainable growth strategies, ensuring BioChroma remains a stable, long-term partner for our clients.

leads.name: Dr. Sarah Jenkins
leads.education: University of North Texas (PhD Organic Chemistry)
leads.expertise: Targeted analysis of vitamins, actives, and degradation products via LC-MS/MS and GC-MS.
leads.deepDive: Our Organic Lead focuses on resolving peak co-elution in complex matrices. By optimizing mobile phase chemistry and utilizing high-resolution mass spectrometry, we ensure that trace-level actives are quantified with absolute specificity.

leads.name: Michael Chen
leads.expertise: Mineral profiling, heavy metals screening, and ICP-MS analytical pathways.
leads.deepDive: Specializing in USP <232>/<233> and California Prop 65 compliance. Our Inorganic team manages the digestion of complex samples—from powders to high-viscosity creams—ensuring detection is accurate at the parts-per-billion level.

leads.name: Elena Rodriguez
leads.expertise: Titrations, physical testing, and compositional analysis (Protein/Nitrogen).
leads.deepDive: The foundation of identity testing. Our Wet Chemistry lead oversees FTIR authentication for raw materials and compendial titrations for bulk material release, providing characterization data required for comprehensive COAs.

leads.name: David Miller
leads.expertise: Oversight of USP <61>/<62> and BAM screening protocols.
leads.deepDive: Ensuring zero-tolerance for pathogens. Our Safety lead manages enrichment protocols designed to detect low-level contaminants, specializing in neutralizer efficacy studies to ensure accurate safety screening.

leads.name: Dr. Linda Voss
leads.expertise: Specialized study design for Antimicrobial Effectiveness (AET) and Time-Kill studies.
leads.deepDive: We help you prove your preservative system works. Following USP <51> guidelines, we measure microbial reduction over a 28-day cycle, providing the data needed for multi-dose product validation.

leads.name: James Wilson
leads.expertise: Management of ICH storage conditions and shelf-life data modeling.
leads.deepDive: Stability is a marathon. Our Lead ensures that environmental chambers remain in constant calibration, managing the pull-schedules and data trending required to defend your product's expiration dating during audits.

leads.name: Rachel Thorne
leads.expertise: Independent data review and ALCOA+ data integrity oversight.
leads.deepDive: The final gatekeeper. Our QC Lead performs a blind technical review of 100% of analytical datasets. By enforcing ALCOA+ principles, we ensure that the data you receive is fully defensible under regulatory scrutiny.

supportAreas.title: Client support model
supportAreas.description: Each project is scoped with clear goals, required deliverables, and a practical communication path so teams know who to contact and what comes next.

supportAreas.title: Scientific leadership in execution
supportAreas.description: Scientific leadership stays involved beyond kickoff by guiding method strategy, resolving technical questions, and aligning decisions with project priorities.

supportAreas.title: Cross-functional coordination
supportAreas.description: Chemistry, microbiology, quality, and support functions coordinate through shared planning and handoffs to keep work moving without losing context.

engagementModes.title: 1. Scope and alignment
engagementModes.description: The team reviews your objectives, matrices, and expected outputs to shape a working plan that is understandable for technical and non-technical stakeholders.

engagementModes.title: 2. Active project coordination
engagementModes.description: Department leads coordinate scheduling, dependencies, and communication so progress remains visible and decisions are made quickly when priorities change.

engagementModes.title: 3. Review and next-step planning
engagementModes.description: Results are delivered with context, discussion of open questions, and recommendations for follow-on work when needed.

supportOutputs.title: Direct access to decision-makers
supportOutputs.description: Clients communicate with people who can answer technical questions and resolve blockers without excessive escalation layers.

supportOutputs.title: Shared understanding across teams
supportOutputs.description: Project communication is designed to keep founders, QA leaders, and product teams aligned on status, risk, and upcoming decisions.

supportOutputs.title: Defensible, practical deliverables
supportOutputs.description: Deliverables are organized for real use in product development and quality workflows, not just as isolated data handoffs.
